tool.name: needle
tool.summary: Compute deterministic global pairwise alignment between exactly one query and one target record

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: `needle` computes a deterministic global pairwise alignment between exactly one query record and one target record. The EMBOSS-RS v1 implementation uses the shared global-alignment core and renders the primary payload as Stockholm.

sections.title: Inputs
sections.content: The current interface accepts two singleton sequence inputs plus optional gap penalties. Multi-record query or target inputs are rejected clearly. Default scoring differs by molecule class: nucleotide mode uses match=1 mismatch=-1 gap_open=5 gap_extend=1, while protein mode uses match=2 mismatch=-1 gap_open=8 gap_extend=1.

sections.title: Outputs
sections.content: The result payload is a pairwise alignment with stable row order `query` then `target`. The CLI renders the payload as Stockholm, and the summary records score, aligned length, identity, and the effective gap penalties.

sections.title: Legacy Context
sections.content: This acceptance anchor keeps one simple historical-style `needle` invocation in view and compares the EMBOSS-RS alignment payload against a committed expected Stockholm rendering. The comparison is intentionally narrow: it validates the governed v1 scoring and rendering path for the committed fixture pair rather than claiming full parameter-surface parity with historical EMBOSS.

sections.title: Current Status
sections.content: This method is implemented and exposed through `emboss-rs needle`. Validation covers execution against committed singleton fixtures, rejection of multi-record query input, and one compared acceptance-anchor case against a committed expected Stockholm output.

sections.title: Caveats
sections.content: The first release is intentionally narrow. `needle` requires exactly one query and one target record, and the compared acceptance case exercises the default nucleotide path only.

artifacts.label: Needle query FASTA fixture
artifacts.description: Repository-managed singleton query fixture for the governed needle acceptance case.

artifacts.label: Needle target FASTA fixture
artifacts.description: Repository-managed singleton target fixture for the governed needle acceptance case.

examples.title: Compute one deterministic global nucleotide alignment
examples.description: Aligns the committed singleton query and target fixtures with the v1 default nucleotide scoring model and renders the primary payload as Stockholm.

expected_outputs.label: Pairwise Stockholm alignment
expected_outputs.description: A stable Stockholm rendering with rows `query` and `target` and one gap in the target row.
